era: 1994-present. Post-Grunge/Rock. E=7 -- arena-scale anthems, survivor's urgency, craft elevated to spectacle.
associated_musicians: Queens of the Stone Age (Grohl guest drums, 2002)

theory_nodes.concept: Drummer becomes frontman. Dave Grohl played every instrument on the 1994 debut tape alone, processing grief after Kurt Cobain's death. The most improbable lead-singer transformation in rock history.
theory_nodes.description: Foo Fighters born from one musician's private grief work.

theory_nodes.concept: Post-grunge formula: quiet-loud dynamics, melodic riffs, mass-participation choruses, vocals pushing to edge of control. Crystallized on The Colour and the Shape (1997). Template for 25 years of commercial rock.
theory_nodes.description: The sonic formula that defined post-grunge as a genre.

theory_nodes.concept: The grief cycle: Cobain (1994) --> Foo Fighters formed. Hawkins (2022) --> But Here We Are (2023). Both times, continuation chosen over dissolution. Survival as consistent creative act.
theory_nodes.description: Grohl's two moments of grief producing creative output.

theory_nodes.concept: Wasting Light (2011): analog tape, Grohl's garage, Butch Vig producing (Nevermind). The deliberate closing of a circle -- same producer, same philosophy, different decade. Critical peak of the catalog.
theory_nodes.description: Wasting Light as the career-spanning artistic statement.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 holds. Progressive Metal to Post-Grunge. Conceptual art-rock to anthemic survivor rock. Bellevue to Capitol Hill.
E=6 drops. Jason Finn (PUSA drummer) was in Love Battery (221) -- the Foo Fighters-adjacent cross-reference closes at 238.

sps_pair.pairing_note: King Salmon meets king of post-grunge. Both 'kings' of their domains. Chinook waterfall leap = massive body-slap at E=6; Foo Fighters E=7 = arena-scale forward momentum. The largest salmon; the largest post-grunge band.

milestones: Post-Grunge/Rock first entry (Foo Fighters is the genre's defining band)
